title: Enforce atomic OSCORE reservations
description: Complete Rust OSCORE crash/concurrency safety with storage-mediated compare-and-swap or an exclusive context-owner lease. Every sequence-consuming public protect path must require a reservation token obtained by atomically advancing persisted state before encryption; Context::new must not let reused key material self-assert freshness; restored ordinary-response one-shot state must be persisted or no-PIV responses prohibited. Remove SecureStack plaintext/mutable-stack bypasses.
design: Reservation storage owns CAS(expected,next) or an exclusive lease. In-memory Context state alone cannot coordinate multiple processes or prove key material is fresh.
acceptance_criteria: Two simulated processes restoring the same expected sequence race and exactly one reserves/transmits; all public request/fresh-PIV response paths require reservation capability; blind-write callbacks reject; crashes after reserve cause gaps not reuse; ordinary response state is persisted or disabled; SecureStack cannot bypass OSCORE through mutable plaintext stack; no_std tests and three security reviews pass.
notes: Completed atomic context-bound CAS reservations, explicit fresh registration versus restore-only loading, crash-safe sequence gaps, transactional ordinary response replay commits, strict RFC option/KID/context handling, authenticated secure ingress, CoAP correlation/ACK handling, and C.4-C.8 vectors. Observe deferred to linked follow-up. Gates: OSCORE all/no-default/Cortex-M, node std, scoped strict Clippy, and three hash-bound security reviews.
close_reason: Atomic OSCORE reservation and secure ingress acceptance complete
